```{r}
surv2 <- read_csv("C:/Users/jc815475/OneDrive - James Cook University/PhD data/3_Coral growth/Data/CSV/survival_transition2.csv", trim_ws=TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| label: survival prep
#| output: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
surv_2 <- surv2 |> 
  mutate(Reef = factor(Reef),
         Site = factor(Site),
         Plot = factor(Plot),
         Colony = factor(Colony),
         Habitat = factor(Habitat),
         Depth = factor(Depth),
         Cross_shelf = factor(Cross_shelf),
         Taxa = factor(Taxa))|> 
  filter(Taxa %in% c("Abra", "Acor", "Pdam", "Pver", "Shys"))

surv_2 <- surv_2 |> rename(Survival = "Survival_2",
                           log_size = "log_2022")

surv_2_sub <- surv_2 |> 
  filter(!Bleaching_scores == "6")

surv_2_sub <- surv_2_sub |> 
  mutate(Bleaching_scores = factor(Bleaching_scores),
         bl_cat1 = fct_recode(Bleaching_scores,
                              `no bleaching` = "1",
                              `minor bleaching` = "2",
                              `major bleaching` = "3",
                              `fully bleached` = "4",
                              `recent partial mortality` = "5"),
         bl_cat2 = fct_recode(Bleaching_scores,
                              `no bleaching` = "1",
                              `partial bleaching` = "2",
                              `partial bleaching` = "3",
                              `fully bleached` = "4",
                              `recent partial mortality` = "5"))
```

For these taxa we decided to filter out colonies smaller than 5 cm in diameter (n = 2163 - 2126 = 37 colonies)

```{r}
#| label: survival prep2
#| output: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

#Filter out colonies that are less than 5cm in diameter
# Area = πr^2
# Where π (pi) is approximately 3.14159, and r is the radius of the circle.
# So if we assume colonies are circle, and we want to filter out the colonies with D < 5 cm, the radius (r) is half of the diameter, so r = 2.5 cm / 2 = 5 cm.
# Calculate the area of a circle R = 2.5 cm:
# Area = π * (2.5 cm)^2
# Area ≈ 78.54 square centimeters
# So, the area of a circle with a diameter of 5 centimeters is approximately 19.6 square centimeters

surv_2_sub <- surv_2_sub |> 
  filter(Area_cm_2022 >= 19.6)

surv_2_sub |> 
  group_by(Taxa) |> 
  summarise(total_count = n()) #lowest sample size is Pdam with 208 colonies
```

## 3.1. Base model for Survival model

### 3.1.a. Acropora staghorn

```{r}
#| label: model1.1.a selection 
Abra_s0 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ log_size : bl_cat1 +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Abra",],
              family = "binomial")

Abra_s1 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 + log_size +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Abra",],
              family = "binomial")

Abra_s2 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Abra",],
              family = "binomial")
```

```{r}
#| label: model1.1.a AIC 
AIC(Abra_s0, Abra_s1, Abra_s2)
```

For *A. branching*, the best-fit model includes the interaction between initial size and bleaching severity.

### 3.1.b. Acropora corymbose

```{r}
#| label: model1.1.b selection 
Acor_s0 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 : log_size +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Acor",],
              family = "binomial")

Acor_s1 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 + log_size +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Acor",],
              family = "binomial")

Acor_s2 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Acor",],
              family = "binomial")
```

```{r}
AIC(Acor_s0, Acor_s1, Acor_s2)
```

For *A. corymbose*, the best-fit model includes initial size and bleaching severity but not their interaction.

### 3.1.c. Pocillopora damicornis

```{r}
#| label: model1.1.c selection
Pdam_s0 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 : log_size +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Pdam",],
              family = "binomial")

Pdam_s1 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 + log_size +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Pdam",],
              family = "binomial")

Pdam_s2 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Pdam",],
              family = "binomial")
```

```{r}
AIC(Pdam_s0, Pdam_s1, Pdam_s2)
```

For *P. damicornis*, the best-fit model includes initial size and bleaching severity but not their interaction.

### 3.1.d. Pocillopora verrucosa

```{r}
#| label: model1.1.d selection
Pver_s0 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 : log_size +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Pver",],
              family = "binomial")
Pver_s1 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 + log_size +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Pver",],
              family = "binomial")
Pver_s2 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Pver",],
              family = "binomial")
```

```{r}
AIC(Pver_s0, Pver_s1, Pver_s2)
```

For *P. verrucosa*, the best-fit model includes initial size and bleaching severity but not their interaction.

### 3.1.e. Seriatopora hystrix

```{r}
#| label: model1.1.e selection
Shys_s0 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 : log_size +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Shys",],
              family = "binomial")
Shys_s1 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 + log_size +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Shys",],
              family = "binomial")
Shys_s2 <- glmmTMB(Survival ~ bl_cat1 +
                (1|Site),
              data = surv_2_sub[surv_2_sub$Taxa == "Shys",],
              family = "binomial")
```

```{r}
AIC(Shys_s0, Shys_s1, Shys_s2)
```

For *S. hystrix*, the best-fit model includes initial size interacting with bleaching severity.

## 3.1.f. Summary figures

```{r}
#| label: model1.1.predictions
#| echo: false
Abra_s0_preds <- predict_response(Abra_s0, terms = c("log_size [all]", "bl_cat1"), margin = "marginalmeans")
Acor_s1_preds <- predict_response(Acor_s1, terms = "log_size [all]", margin = "marginalmeans")
Pdam_s1_preds <- predict_response(Pdam_s1, terms = c("log_size [all]"), margin = "marginalmeans")
Pver_s1_preds <- predict_response(Pver_s1, terms = c("log_size [all]"), margin = "marginalmeans")
Shys_s0_preds <- predict_response(Shys_s0, terms = c("log_size [all]", "bl_cat1"), margin = "marginalmeans")
```

```{r}
#| label: model1.1.predictions cont.
#| echo: false
Abra_s0_preds$Taxa = "Abra"
Pdam_s1_preds$Taxa = "Pdam"
Pver_s1_preds$Taxa = "Pver"
Acor_s1_preds$Taxa = "Acor"
Shys_s0_preds$Taxa = "Shys"

group_int <- rbind(Abra_s0_preds, Shys_s0_preds)
group_no_int <- rbind(Acor_s1_preds, Pdam_s1_preds, Pver_s1_preds)
```

```{r}
#| label: model1.1. pred for bl_cat
#| echo: false
#| results: false

surv_bl_preds1 <- predict_response(Acor_s1, terms = "bl_cat1 [all]", margin = "marginalmeans")
surv_bl_preds1$Taxa = "Acor"
surv_bl_preds2 <- predict_response(Pdam_s1, terms = "bl_cat1 [all]", margin = "marginalmeans")
surv_bl_preds2$Taxa = "Pdam"
surv_bl_preds3 <- predict_response(Pver_s1, terms = "bl_cat1 [all]", margin = "marginalmeans")
surv_bl_preds3$Taxa = "Pver"
surv_bl_preds <- rbind(surv_bl_preds1, surv_bl_preds2, surv_bl_preds3)

surv_bl_preds |> 
  mutate(x = factor(x, levels = c("no bleaching", "minor bleaching", "major bleaching", "fully bleached", "recent partial mortality")))
surv_bl_preds |> mutate(Taxa = factor(Taxa))
```

```{r}
#| label: model1.1. figure
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 10

taxa_labels1 <- c("Abra" = "A. branching",
                 "Shys" = "S. hystrix")

taxa_labels2 <- c("Acor" = "A. corymbose",
                 "Pdam" = "P. damicornis",
                 "Pver" = "P. verrucosa")

colours_sev = c("#F0E442",  "#E69F00", "#D55E00", "red", "black")

surv_plotA <- ggplot(group_int, aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high, fill = group), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~Taxa, labeller =  as_labeller(taxa_labels1), ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colours_sev) +
  scale_color_manual(values = colours_sev) +
  labs(title = "A) ", 
       x = "Log Area 2022", 
       y = "Survival Probability", 
       color = "Bleaching Category", 
       fill = "Bleaching Category") +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("initial size (log)") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 12),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linetype = "dashed"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.position = "none"
  )

surv_plotB <- ggplot(group_no_int, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "blue") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~Taxa, labeller =  as_labeller(taxa_labels2), ) +
  labs(title = "B) ", 
       x = "initial size (log)", 
       y = "") +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.25)) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 12),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linetype = "dashed"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )

surv_plotC <- ggplot()+
  geom_errorbar(data = surv_bl_preds, aes(x = x, ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high, 
                                   group = Taxa), width = 0.1, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(data = surv_bl_preds, aes(x = x, y = predicted, fill = x, group = Taxa),
             size = 4, pch = 21) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = colours_sev,
                      name = "") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colours_sev,
                    name = "") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.25)) +
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~ Taxa, labeller =  as_labeller(taxa_labels2), )+
  labs(title = "C)", 
       x = "", 
       y = "Survival probability") +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linetype = "dashed"),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = levels(surv_bl_preds$x))

surv_plotA + surv_plotB + surv_plotC + plot_layout(ncol = 2)

```

## 3.2. Data exploration - raw data plotting

### 3.2.a. Mortality rates pre- and post-bleaching (raw data plotting)

```{r}
#| label: Barplot1
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 10

surv1_summary <- surv_1 |>
  group_by(Taxa) |>
  summarise(
    dead_count = sum(Survival_1 == 0),
    alive_count = sum(Survival_1 == 1),
    total_count = n(),
    dead_proportion = dead_count / total_count,
    alive_proportion = alive_count / total_count,
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(dead_proportion))


surv1_long <- surv1_summary |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(dead_proportion, alive_proportion), 
               names_to = "status", 
               values_to = "proportion")


surv1_long <- surv1_long |>
  group_by(Taxa) |>
  mutate(cumulative = cumsum(proportion))

surv1_long <- surv1_long |> 
  mutate(Taxa = factor(Taxa, levels = c("Abra", "Acor", "Pdam", "Pver","Shys")))

mort1 <- ggplot(surv1_long, aes(x = Taxa, y = proportion, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("dead_proportion" = "black", "alive_proportion" = "grey"), 
                    labels = c("dead_proportion" = "Dead", "alive_proportion" = "Alive")) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Proportion of colonies", fill = "Survival", title = "A - year 1 (no bleaching)") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(proportion, accuracy = 0.1), 
                y = cumulative - proportion / 2), 
            color = "white") +
  theme_bw() +
    scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Abra", "Acor", "Pdam", "Pver","Shys"),
                   labels = c("Acropora branching",
                              "Acropora corymbose",
                              "Pocillopora damicornis",
                              "Pocillopora verrucosa",
                              "Seriatopora hystrix")) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )



surv2_summary <- surv_2 |>
  group_by(Taxa) |>
  summarise(
    dead_count = sum(Survival_2 == 0),
    alive_count = sum(Survival_2 == 1),
    total_count = n(),
    dead_proportion = dead_count / total_count,
    alive_proportion = alive_count / total_count,
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(dead_proportion))


surv2_long <- surv2_summary |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(dead_proportion, alive_proportion), 
               names_to = "status", 
               values_to = "proportion")


surv2_long <- surv2_long |>
  group_by(Taxa) |>
  mutate(cumulative = cumsum(proportion))

surv2_long <- surv2_long |> 
  mutate(Taxa = factor(Taxa, levels = c("Abra", "Acor", "Pdam", "Pver","Shys")))

mort2 <- ggplot(surv2_long, aes(x = Taxa, y = proportion, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("dead_proportion" = "black", "alive_proportion" = "grey"), 
                    labels = c("dead_proportion" = "Dead", "alive_proportion" = "Alive")) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Proportion of colonies", fill = "Survival", title = "B - year 2 (bleaching)") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(proportion, accuracy = 0.1), 
                y = cumulative - proportion / 2), 
            color = "white") +
  theme_bw() +
    scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Abra", "Acor", "Pdam", "Pver","Shys"),
                   labels = c("Acropora branching",
                              "Acropora corymbose",
                              "Pocillopora damicornis",
                              "Pocillopora verrucosa",
                              "Seriatopora hystrix")) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

mort1 + mort2 + plot_layout(ncol = 1)

```

Mortality increased after bleaching for all taxa.

### 3.2.b. Survival rates per bleaching category (raw data plotting)

```{r}
#| label: survHeatmap
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 8

surv_2_sub <- surv_2 |> 
  filter(!Bleaching_scores == "6")

taxa_labels <- c("Abra" = "Acropora branching",
                 "Acor" = "Acropora corymbose",
                 "Pdam" = "Pocillopora damicornis",
                 "Pver" = "Pocillopora verrucosa",
                 "Shys" = "Seriatopora hystrix")

bl_cat <- c("1" = "No bleaching",
            "2" = "Minor bleaching",
            "3" = "Major bleaching",
            "4" = "Fully bleached",
            "5" = "Recent partial mortality")

Taxa_sum2 <- surv_2_sub |> 
  group_by(Taxa, Bleaching_scores) |> 
  summarize(Survival_Rate2 = mean(Survival_2),
            Colony_count2 = n())

ggplot(Taxa_sum2, aes(x = factor(Bleaching_scores), y = Taxa, fill = Survival_Rate2)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Colony_count2), color = "white", size = 5) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "black", high = "grey", limits = c(min_surv, max_surv)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = c("Shys", "Pver","Pdam","Acor","Abra"),
                   labels = c("Seriatopora hystrix",
                              "Pocillopora verrucosa",
                              "Pocillopora damicornis",
                              "Acropora corymbose",
                              "Acropora branching")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = bl_cat) +
  labs(x = "", 
       y = "", 
       fill = "Survival Rate") +
  theme_classic()+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, angle = 90),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size =12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )
```

### 3.2.c. Growth rates pre- and post-bleaching (all colonies)

```{r}
#| label: growfig1
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 16
gt1 <- grow1 |> ggplot(
  aes(x = log10_2021, y = log10_2022, group = Taxa)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, pch = 16) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "deepskyblue")+
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "A - Year 1 (pre-bleaching)",
       x = "Size in 2021 (log)",
       y = "Size in 2022 (log)") +
  # scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 12), breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 2)) +
  # scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 12), breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 2)) +
  facet_wrap(~Taxa, scales = "free", labeller =  as_labeller(taxa_labels), ) +
  theme_bw()

gt2 <- grow2 |> ggplot(
  aes(x = log10_2022, y = log10_2023, group = Taxa)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, pch = 16) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "darkorange")+
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0) +
  labs(title = "B - Year 2 (post-bleaching)",
       x = "Size in 2022 (log)",
       y = "Size in 2023 (log)") +
  # scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 12), breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 2)) +
  # scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 12), breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 2)) +
  facet_wrap(~Taxa, scales = "free", labeller =  as_labeller(taxa_labels), ) +
  theme_bw()

gt1 + gt2 + plot_layout(ncol = 1)
```

The dashed line represents the no-growth line (intercept 0, slope 1). All slopes are \< 1 meaning growth rates decreases with size. It looks like in general in year 2, the curves are closer to the no-growth line.

### 3.2.d. Growth rates pre- and post-bleaching (healthy colonies only)

```{r}
#| label: healthydataprep
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| results: hide
rawdata <- read_csv("C:/Users/jc815475/OneDrive - James Cook University/PhD data/3_Coral growth/Data/CSV/growth_surv_data.csv", trim_ws=TRUE)

dat1 <- rawdata |> 
  mutate(Taxa_2023 = factor(Class_2023),
         Taxa_2023 = fct_recode(Taxa_2023,
                                Atab = "Acyt",
                                Atab = "Ahya",
                                Abra = "Asta",
                                Adgt = "Agem",
                                Adgt = "Ahum",
                                Acor = "Amil",
                                Acor = "Aten",
                                Gspp = "Gedw",
                                Gspp = "Gret"),
         Taxa_2022 = factor(Class_2022),
         Taxa_2022 = fct_recode(Taxa_2022,
                                Atab = "Acyt",
                                Atab = "Ahya",
                                Abra = "Asta",
                                Adgt = "Agem",
                                Adgt = "Ahum",
                                Acor = "Amil",
                                Acor = "Aten",
                                Gspp = "Gedw",
                                Gspp = "Gret"),
         Taxa_2021 = factor(Class_2021),
         Taxa_2021 = fct_recode(Taxa_2021,
                                Atab = "Acyt",
                                Atab = "Ahya",
                                Abra = "Asta",
                                Adgt = "Agem",
                                Adgt = "Ahum",
                                Acor = "Amil",
                                Acor = "Aten",
                                Gspp = "Gedw",
                                Gspp = "Gret"))

### Subset data for each transition
healthy <- dat1 |> 
  filter(to_use_0 == "yes" & to_use_growth_1 == "yes" & to_use_growth_2 == "yes")

### Only retain taxa of interest
healthy <- healthy |> 
  filter(Taxa_2023 %in% c("Abra", "Acor", "Pdam", "Pver", "Shys")) |> 
  droplevels()

healthy <- healthy |> 
  filter(Taxa_2022 %in% c("Abra", "Acor", "Pdam", "Pver", "Shys")) |> 
  droplevels()

healthy <- healthy |> 
  filter(Taxa_2021 %in% c("Abra", "Acor", "Pdam", "Pver", "Shys")) |> 
  droplevels()

### only retain rows where Taxa match throughout the years
healthy <- healthy[healthy$Taxa_2021 == healthy$Taxa_2022 & healthy$Taxa_2022 == healthy$Taxa_2023, ]

### Check taxa levels for each year
levels(healthy$Taxa_2021)
levels(healthy$Taxa_2022)
levels(healthy$Taxa_2023)

### only retain colonies that stayed healthy in Year 2
healthy <- healthy |> 
  mutate(Bleaching_scores = factor(Bleaching_scores)) |> 
  filter(Bleaching_scores == "1")

## Setup hierarchical design
healthy <- healthy |> 
  mutate(Plot = factor(Plot_ID),
         Zone = factor(str_sub(Plot, 9,9)),
         Reef = factor(str_sub(Plot, 1,4)),
         Site = factor(str_sub(Plot, 1,9)), #unique combination of Reef/Site/Zone
         Site_type = factor(str_sub(Plot, 6, 7)),
         Colony_ID = row_number(),
         Colony = factor(paste(Plot, Colony_ID, sep = "_")),
         Habitat = factor(paste(Site_type, Zone, sep = "_")),
         Reef = fct_recode(Reef,
                           PA = "PAPE",
                           PA = "PAOR"),
         Depth = fct_recode(Zone,
                            Deep = "D",
                            Shallow = "S"),
         Area_cm_2021 = Area_2021*10000, #change area in m2 to cm2
         Area_cm_2022 = Area_2022*10000,
         Area_cm_2023 = Area_2023*10000,
         log_2021= log(Area_cm_2021),
         log_2022= log(Area_cm_2022),
         log_2023= log(Area_cm_2023),
         log10_2021=log10(Area_cm_2021),
         log10_2022 =log10(Area_cm_2022),
         log10_2023 =log10(Area_cm_2023),
         Taxa = factor(Taxa_2021),
         Cross_shelf = fct_recode(Reef,
                                  offshore = "OCCH",
                                  offshore = "OCDA",
                                  offshore = "OCLB",
                                  inshore = "PA"))

healthy <- healthy |> 
  select(Reef, Site, Plot, Colony, Habitat, Depth, Cross_shelf, Taxa, Area_cm_2021, Area_cm_2022, Area_cm_2023, log_2021, log_2022, log_2023, log10_2021, log10_2022, log10_2023, Note_2021, Note_2022, Note_2023)

```

```{r}
#| label: healthyrawfig
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 10
# Pivot longer to reshape the dataset for modeling
healthy_long <- healthy |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = matches(".*_[0-9]{4}"),  
               names_to = c(".value", "Year"), 
               names_pattern = "(.+)_(.+)")    

healthy_long <- healthy_long |> 
  mutate(Year = factor(Year),
         Taxa = factor(Taxa))


# Plot raw data
data_2021_2022 <- healthy |>
  select(Taxa, log10_2021, log10_2022) |>
  rename(log_previous = log10_2021, log_size = log10_2022) |>
  mutate(Year_transition = "2021-2022")

data_2022_2023 <- healthy |>
  select(Taxa, log10_2022, log10_2023) |>
  rename(log_previous = log10_2022, log_size = log10_2023) |>
  mutate(Year_transition = "2022-2023")

plot_data <- bind_rows(data_2021_2022, data_2022_2023)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = log_previous, y = log_size, color = Year_transition)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +  
  facet_wrap(~ Taxa, scales = "free", labeller =  as_labeller(taxa_labels)) +  
  labs(x = "log(size at t previous)", y = "log(size at t)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("2021-2022" = "cadetblue", "2022-2023" = "#D55E00"))
```

### 3.2.f. Growth per taxa and bleaching category

```{r}
#| label: growfig2
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 10
grow2 <- grow2 |> 
  mutate(bl_cat1 = case_when(
    is.na(Bleaching_scores)  ~ "no bleaching",
    Bleaching_scores == 1   ~ "no bleaching",
    Bleaching_scores == 2   ~ "minor bleaching",
    Bleaching_scores == 3   ~ "major bleaching",
    Bleaching_scores == 4   ~ "fully bleached",
    Bleaching_scores == 5   ~ "recent partial mortality"
  ))

grow2 <- grow2 |> 
  mutate(bl_cat1 = factor(bl_cat1, levels = c("no bleaching", "minor bleaching", "major bleaching", "fully bleached", "recent partial mortality")))

colours_bl = c("#F0E442","#E69F00", "#D55E00" ,"#990000", "black")

grow2 |> ggplot(
  aes(x = log10_2022, y = log10_2023, group = Taxa, colour = bl_cat1)) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.4, height = 0.4), alpha = 0.2, pch = 16) +
  scale_color_manual(values = colours_bl) +
  labs(x = "Size in 2022 (log)",
       y = "Size in 2023 (log)") +
  # scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 12), breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 2)) +
  # scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 12), breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 2)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = bl_cat1, colour = bl_cat1), method = "lm", se = FALSE, size = 1.2)+
  facet_wrap(~Taxa, scales = "free", labeller =  as_labeller(taxa_labels), ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )
```
